overview.what: Implement a simple linear regression model from scratch using gradient descent. This project helps visualize how a model 'learns' a line of best fit for a set of data points.
overview.how_it_works: We use the Mean Squared Error (MSE) loss function and calculate gradients to iteratively update the slope and intercept parameters. This minimizes the error between predictions and actual targets.

files.description: Pure Python implementation of the Linear Regression class.
files.code:
import numpy as np

class LinearRegression:
    def __init__(self, lr=0.01, iterations=1000):
        self.lr = lr
        self.iterations = iterations
        self.w = None
        self.b = None

    def fit(self, X, y):
        n_samples, n_features = X.shape
        self.w = np.zeros(n_features)
        self.b = 0

        for _ in range(self.iterations):
            y_pred = np.dot(X, self.w) + self.b
            
            dw = (1 / n_samples) * np.dot(X.T, (y_pred - y))
            db = (1 / n_samples) * np.sum(y_pred - y)

            self.w -= self.lr * dw
            self.b -= self.lr * db

    def predict(self, X):
        return np.dot(X, self.w) + self.b
